description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 5 test-level cluster: scripted browser E2E vs integration (no browser) vs unit vs contract, plus the permanent-suite vs one-off-walkthrough boundary with clickthrough-test-engineer.

cases.prompt: Automate the checkout journey in a real browser as a permanent regression test with traces on failure.
cases.reason: A permanent scripted browser test for a critical journey is exactly E2E engineering — not a one-off walkthrough, not a no-browser boundary test.

cases.prompt: Before the demo tomorrow, click through the whole app once and tell me what's broken.
cases.reason: A one-time interactive walkthrough with observations is clickthrough testing; building permanent specs for it would be the wrong deliverable.

cases.prompt: Exercise the payments command through the real service and database with real permission checks.
cases.reason: Real boundaries without a UI journey is the integration layer — cheaper and more reliable than E2E for this risk.

cases.prompt: Verify the webhook payloads we send still match the documented schema consumers rely on.
cases.reason: Payload shape/compatibility verification is contract testing; no browser journey exists at all.
